title: Arthraxon submuticus
intro: Arthraxon submuticus, also known as Japanese hairy grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Arthraxon submuticus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Arthraxon submuticus is typically prepared as a decoction or a tea, which is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Arthraxon submuticus is classified within the family Poaceae and the genus Arthraxon. The Poaceae family is commonly known as the grass family. This species is closely related to other members of the Arthraxon genus, which also consists of grasses.
